body.title: Web server: incoming poll queue not filtered by agent_id
body.result: per-agent queues: _api_agent_poll drains agent-specific queue; _api_cc_send routes to to_agent param; file uploads go to igor
body.intention: I intend that each agent's poll endpoint returns only messages intended for that agent, preventing message theft between co-registered agents.
body.description:
GET /api/agents/{id}/poll drains the global `incoming` queue without filtering on agent_id (server.py:_api_agent_poll). With igor+librarian+granny-weatherwax all registered, whichever agent polls first consumes all pending messages. Currently only Igor polls, so it's not triggering — but as more devices poll, message theft will occur.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (_api_agent_poll, incoming queue)

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** Add agent_id filtering to poll endpoint; route messages to per-agent queues or add agent_id field to incoming messages. Out of scope: changing WS message routing.

**Completion criteria:** POST /api/agents/igor/send + GET /api/agents/librarian/poll returns empty; message only returned by GET /api/agents/igor/poll.
